_metadata.notes: Insurance penetration rates and premiums are approximate and based on latest available data (2023-2024). Per capita figures in USD.

mandatoryInsurance.description: All vehicles on Nigerian roads must carry third-party insurance
Employers with 5+ employees must maintain life insurance for employees (min 3x annual salary)
Buildings over 2 floors require insurance against construction risks and third-party liability
Public buildings must insure against risks to third parties on premises
Employers must contribute to Employee Compensation Fund via NSITF
Medical practitioners must carry professional indemnity insurance
All imports into Nigeria must be insured with Nigerian insurers (local content requirement)
Operators must insure all insurable risks with locally registered insurers

classes.name: Third-Party Only
classes.description: Covers death, bodily injury, and property damage to third parties
classes.minimumCover: Bodily injury: unlimited; Property damage: NGN 1,000,000

classes.name: Third-Party, Fire & Theft
classes.description: Third-party cover plus own vehicle fire and theft coverage

classes.name: Comprehensive
classes.description: Full cover including own vehicle damage, third-party, fire, theft, natural disasters
classes.annualPremium: 3-5% of vehicle value (e.g. NGN 150,000-250,000 for a NGN 5M car)
classes.minimumCover: Third-party property: NGN 5-25 million depending on insurer

noClaimsDiscount.notes: Varies by insurer; some offer graduated scales

steps: Report incident to police and obtain case number
Notify insurer within stipulated time (usually 24-48 hours)
Complete claim form and submit with supporting documents
Insurer appoints loss adjuster for assessment

marineCargo.notes: All imports must be insured with Nigerian-registered insurers (local content requirement)
marineCargo.rateRange: 0.1-2.0% of cargo value depending on goods type and route

keyLaws: Social Health Insurance Act 2023 (SHIF replacing NHIF)

mandatoryInsurance.description: Required for all motor vehicles under Traffic Act CAP 403
All employers must insure against work injury benefits
Social Health Insurance Fund mandatory for all employed persons; employers must match contributions
Required for insurance intermediaries, medical practitioners
Imports above certain threshold must be insured locally

classes.name: Third-Party Only
classes.description: Covers injury/death and property damage to third parties
classes.minimumCover: KES 20 million bodily injury; KES 2 million property

classes.name: Third-Party, Fire & Theft
classes.description: Third-party plus fire and theft of own vehicle

classes.name: Comprehensive
classes.description: All-risk cover including own damage

noClaimsDiscount.notes: Most insurers offer NCD; transferable between insurers with proof

microinsurance.notes: M-PESA widely used for premium collection; Bima ya Mwananchi health product notable

steps: Report to police (accident/theft) and obtain abstract
Fill claim form (available online from most insurers)
Settlement via EFT or repair authorization
requiredDocuments: Driver's license of person driving at time

marineCargo.notes: IRA requires all imports to be insured locally

regulatory.body: Prudential Authority (PA) & Financial Sector Conduct Authority (FSCA)
keyLaws: Compensation for Occupational Injuries and Diseases Act (COIDA)

mandatoryInsurance.description: Not traditional motor insurance; funded via fuel levy. Covers all road users for bodily injury/death. Motor vehicle insurance is NOT compulsory in SA since 1997.
All employers must register and contribute to Compensation Fund for occupational injuries/diseases
Mandatory contributions from employers and employees (1% each of salary)
Attorneys, auditors, medical practitioners, etc. must carry PI cover
Commercial flight operators must carry liability insurance
Certain industries must insure environmental damage risks

classes.name: Third-Party Only
classes.description: Covers damage to other people's property/vehicles; NOT legally required but widely recommended

classes.name: Third-Party, Fire & Theft
classes.description: Third-party plus fire and theft cover for own vehicle

classes.name: Comprehensive
classes.description: Full cover for own vehicle and third-party damages
classes.annualPremium: ZAR 600-2,500/month depending on vehicle value and driver profile

noClaimsDiscount.maximumDiscount: Up to 70% (some insurers like OUTsurance offer cash back after 3 years)
noClaimsDiscount.notes: Major feature of SA motor market; OUTsurance OUTbonus pays 10% back every 3 claim-free years

motorInsurance.importantNote: Motor vehicle insurance is NOT compulsory in South Africa. The RAF (funded by fuel levy) covers bodily injury. Property damage is NOT covered by RAF.

microinsurance.notes: Funeral cover dominates SA microinsurance; burial societies widespread

steps: Ensure safety and report to SAPS (police) to obtain case number
Complete claim form with incident details
Settlement via approved panel repairer or cash payout
claimProcess.averageProcessingDays: 7-30 days (SA has fastest claim processing in Africa)

marineCargo.notes: Not legally required but strongly recommended. CIF imports usually include insurance.

mandatoryInsurance.description: All vehicles must carry third-party insurance. Motor Insurance Database (MID) tracks compliance.
Mandatory for formal sector workers (SSNIT contributors); voluntary for informal sector
Employers must compensate for work-related injuries and diseases
Commercial premises and certain buildings require fire insurance

classes.name: Third-Party Only
classes.description: Covers injury/death and property damage to third parties; legally required
classes.annualPremium: GHS 530 for private cars (2025 rate); GHS 637 for taxis

classes.name: Third-Party, Fire & Theft
classes.description: Adds fire and theft cover for own vehicle

classes.name: Comprehensive
classes.description: Full coverage for own vehicle and third parties

microinsurance.notes: Tigo/BIMA/MicroEnsure freemium model pioneered in Ghana; loyalty-based insurance where airtime users get free cover

steps: Report to Ghana Police Service and obtain report
Submit police report, medical report, 2 passport photos

marineCargo.notes: Insurance Act requires certain imports to be insured locally

keyLaws: Law No. 7 of 2007 (compulsory motor insurance, now subsumed)

mandatoryInsurance.description: All vehicles must carry third-party civil liability insurance
All employed persons covered under national social insurance scheme
Unified Insurance Law empowers FRA to mandate coverage for doctors, lawyers, accountants
Insurance for government-owned assets proposed under new law

classes.name: Third-Party Only (Compulsory)
classes.description: Covers bodily injury and death to third parties caused by vehicle

classes.name: Comprehensive
classes.description: Own vehicle damage plus third-party cover

microinsurance.notes: Microinsurance growing but less developed than East/West Africa mobile models

steps: Report to police and obtain report

marineCargo.notes: Imports typically require local insurance under trade regulations

regulatory.body: National Bank of Ethiopia (Insurance Supervision Directorate)
keyLaws: Directive SIB/60/2023 (Motor Insurance Minimum Premium Rate)

mandatoryInsurance.description: All motor vehicles must carry third-party insurance. Premiums significantly raised in 2023 (up to 5x previous rates).
Insurance brokers and agents must carry PI cover

classes.name: Third-Party Only
classes.description: Mandatory cover for bodily injury and property damage to third parties

classes.name: Comprehensive
classes.description: Full cover including own vehicle

microinsurance.notes: Index-based livestock insurance (IBLI) and crop insurance have had donor-supported pilots

mandatoryInsurance.description: All motor vehicles must carry third-party liability insurance
Employers must insure employees against work-related injuries

classes.name: Third-Party Only
classes.description: Covers third-party bodily injury/death and property damage

classes.name: Third-Party, Fire & Theft
classes.description: Adds fire and theft cover for own vehicle

classes.name: Comprehensive
classes.description: Full coverage

keyLaws: Motor Vehicle Insurance (Third Party Risks) Act CAP 214

mandatoryInsurance.description: All motor vehicles must carry third-party insurance; digital stickers being rolled out
Employers must compensate workers for occupational injuries

classes.name: Third-Party Only
classes.description: Covers third-party bodily injury/death and property damage

classes.name: Third-Party, Fire & Theft
classes.description: Plus fire and theft for own vehicle

classes.name: Comprehensive
classes.description: Full cover including own vehicle

regulatory.body: National Bank of Rwanda (Insurance Supervision Department)
keyLaws: Law No. 30/2021 Governing Organisation of Insurance Business
Law on Compulsory Motor Vehicle Insurance
Community Based Health Insurance (Mutuelle de Sante) Law

mandatoryInsurance.description: All vehicles must carry third-party insurance
MANDATORY for all residents. Community-based health insurance covering 85%+ of population. Premiums vary by income category.
Employers must insure against occupational risks

classes.name: Third-Party Only
classes.description: Covers third-party injury and property damage

classes.name: Comprehensive
classes.description: Full coverage including own vehicle

microinsurance.notes: Rwanda is a global leader in universal health coverage through its Mutuelle de Sante program

mandatoryInsurance.description: Mandatory for all vehicles under CIMA Code Article 200
Insurance brokers/agents must carry PI cover
All-risks and ten-year liability for construction projects
Employers must insure against occupational accidents

classes.name: Third-Party Only (Responsabilite Civile)
classes.description: Covers damage/injury to third parties; mandatory

classes.name: Third-Party, Fire & Theft
classes.description: Adds fire and theft cover

classes.name: Comprehensive (Tous Risques)
classes.description: Full coverage

penetration.notes: Largest insurance market in CIMA zone

regulatory.body: CIMA (regional) / Ministere des Finances, Direction des Assurances (national)

mandatoryInsurance.description: Compulsory under CIMA Code Article 200
Required for insurance brokers/agents
Employers must insure occupational risks

penetration.notes: Second-largest market in CIMA zone after Cote d'Ivoire

keyLaws: National Insurance Regulations under Ministry of Finance

mandatoryInsurance.description: All vehicles must have third-party insurance; policies must include RC (Responsabilite Civile)
Compulsory for registered vessels
Insurance intermediaries must carry PI cover
Employers must insure occupational risks

penetration.notes: Third-largest non-life market in CIMA zone

regulatory.body: Autorite de Controle des Assurances et de la Prevoyance Sociale

mandatoryInsurance.description: Mandatory under Article 120 of Insurance Code for all vehicle owners
Employers must insure against occupational accidents and diseases
Required for notaries, architects, accountants, and others
Hunters must carry third-party liability insurance
Builders must carry 10-year structural liability insurance

classes.name: Third-Party Only (RC Obligatoire)
classes.description: Mandatory civil liability cover

classes.name: Third-Party, Fire & Theft
classes.description: RC plus fire and theft for own vehicle

classes.name: Comprehensive (Tous Risques)
classes.description: Full coverage

penetration.notes: Second-largest insurance market in Africa by premium volume

microinsurance.notes: MAMDA is Africa's largest agricultural mutual insurer; Takaful (Islamic) microinsurance growing

mandatoryInsurance.description: Mandatory for all vehicles; largest insurance product by premium
Compulsory for aircraft operators
Required for vessel operators
Required for insurance intermediaries and certain professions
Mandatory social security contributions for all employed

classes.name: Third-Party Only (RC Obligatoire)
classes.description: Mandatory civil liability

classes.name: Comprehensive (Tous Risques)
classes.description: Full coverage

penetration.notes: Automobile insurance is 39% of total premium revenue (TND 1.5 billion in 2024)

providers: STAR (Societe Tunisienne d'Assurances et de Reassurances)

AO.name: Angola

regulatory.body: Agencia Angolana de Regulacao e Supervisao de Seguros
keyLaws: Law on Insurance and Reinsurance Activity (2022)

mandatoryInsurance.description: All motor vehicles must carry third-party insurance
Employers must insure against occupational accidents - dominant compulsory class
Aircraft operators must carry liability insurance

classes.name: Third-Party Only
classes.description: Covers third-party bodily injury and property damage

classes.name: Comprehensive
classes.description: Full coverage

microinsurance.notes: Microinsurance market is nascent in Angola; limited providers

majorInsurers: ENSA (Empresa Nacional de Seguros de Angola - state-owned, largest)

DZ.name: Algeria
DZ.regulatoryBody: Conseil National des Assurances (CNA) / Commission de Supervision des Assurances (CSA)
DZ.notes: Motor insurance accounts for 56.8% of non-life premiums. Orange Card scheme for cross-border motor insurance.

BJ.name: Benin
BJ.regulatoryBody: CIMA / Direction des Assurances (Ministry of Finance)
BJ.notes: CIMA member state; ECOWAS Brown Card for cross-border motor insurance

BW.name: Botswana
BW.notes: Relatively well-developed market for Southern Africa

BF.name: Burkina Faso
BF.regulatoryBody: CIMA / Direction des Assurances (Ministry of Economy & Finance)

BI.name: Burundi
BI.regulatoryBody: Banque de la Republique du Burundi (BRB) / Direction des Assurances
BI.notes: COMESA Yellow Card scheme for cross-border motor insurance

CV.name: Cabo Verde
CV.notes: Small island market; Portuguese-language legal framework

CF.name: Central African Republic
CF.notes: CIMA member state; very low insurance development

TD.name: Chad
TD.regulatoryBody: CIMA / Direction des Assurances (Ministry of Finance)

KM.name: Comoros
KM.notes: Smallest insurance market in Africa; limited regulatory capacity

CD.name: DR Congo
CD.regulatoryBody: ARCA (Autorite de Regulation et de Controle des Assurances)
CD.notes: Market liberalized in 2015 after decades of state monopoly (SONAS); Law 15/005 of 2015 (Insurance Code)

DJ.name: Djibouti
DJ.regulatoryBody: Ministere de l'Economie et des Finances

ER.name: Eritrea
ER.regulatoryBody: Ministry of Finance / National Insurance Corporation
majorInsurers: National Insurance Corporation of Eritrea (NICE - state-owned)
ER.notes: State-dominated insurance market; very limited private sector

SZ.name: Eswatini
SZ.notes: Small but relatively developed market influenced by South Africa

GA.name: Gabon
GA.notes: CIMA headquarters (CRCA) located in Libreville, Gabon

GM.name: Gambia
GM.regulatoryBody: Central Bank of The Gambia (Insurance Division)
GM.notes: Small English-speaking market in West Africa

GN.name: Guinea
GN.regulatoryBody: CIMA / Banque Centrale de la Republique de Guinee
majorInsurers: UGAR (Union Guineenne d'Assurances et de Reassurances)

GW.name: Guinea-Bissau
GW.notes: CIMA member since 2002; very small market

LS.name: Lesotho
LS.regulatoryBody: Central Bank of Lesotho (Insurance Supervision Department)
LS.notes: Heavily influenced by South African insurance market

LR.name: Liberia
LR.notes: Post-conflict recovery market; motor third-party compulsory

LY.name: Libya
LY.notes: Market disrupted by political instability; state-owned companies dominate

MG.name: Madagascar
MG.regulatoryBody: Ministere des Finances / Direction des Assurances
MG.notes: Former CIMA observer; separate regulatory framework

MW.name: Malawi
MW.notes: Insurance Act 2010; COMESA Yellow Card scheme

ML.name: Mali
ML.regulatoryBody: CIMA / Direction des Assurances (Ministry of Finance)

MR.name: Mauritania
MR.regulatoryBody: Direction de Controle des Assurances (Ministry of Finance)
MR.notes: Very small market; not CIMA member

MU.name: Mauritius
MU.notes: Well-regulated market; second-highest penetration in Africa after South Africa

MZ.name: Mozambique
MZ.regulatoryBody: Instituto de Supervisao de Seguros de Mocambique (ISSM)
MZ.notes: Third-party insurance required for all vehicles including foreign-registered at border

NA.name: Namibia
NA.notes: Third-highest insurance penetration in Africa; heavily influenced by South African market

NE.name: Niger
NE.regulatoryBody: CIMA / Direction des Assurances (Ministry of Finance)
NE.notes: CIMA member state; one of lowest penetration rates in Africa

SL.name: Sierra Leone
SL.notes: Post-conflict market; ECOWAS Brown Card scheme

SO.name: Somalia
SO.regulatoryBody: Ministry of Finance (no dedicated insurance regulator) / Somaliland: National Insurance Authority (NIA)
SO.notes: Virtually no formal insurance market; Somaliland has separate nascent regulatory framework; Islamic finance principles

SS.name: South Sudan
SS.notes: Newest country in Africa; extremely nascent insurance market

SD.name: Sudan
SD.notes: Takaful (Islamic) insurance dominant; market affected by sanctions history

ST.name: Sao Tome and Principe
ST.regulatoryBody: Banco Central de Sao Tome e Principe
ST.notes: Smallest insurance market in Africa by population

TG.name: Togo
TG.regulatoryBody: CIMA / Direction des Assurances (Ministry of Economy & Finance)
TG.notes: CIMA member state; ECOWAS Brown Card scheme

ZM.name: Zambia
ZM.notes: New Insurance Act took effect 2021; COMESA Yellow Card scheme

ZW.name: Zimbabwe
ZW.notes: Relatively high penetration for sub-Saharan Africa; Road Traffic Act mandates motor third-party

ECOWAS_BrownCard.name: ECOWAS Brown Card
ECOWAS_BrownCard.description: Cross-border motor vehicle third-party liability insurance scheme for ECOWAS member states

COMESA_YellowCard.name: COMESA Yellow Card
COMESA_YellowCard.description: Cross-border motor vehicle insurance scheme for COMESA/EAC member states

CIMA.name: CIMA (Conference Interafricaine des Marches d'Assurances)
CIMA.description: Regional insurance regulatory body with unified insurance code for 14/15 francophone African countries
CIMA.supervisoryBody: CRCA (Commission Regionale de Controle des Assurances)
keyFeatures: Unified CIMA Code governs all insurance
Motor third-party mandatory in all member states (Article 200)
Insurance companies from member states can operate across CIMA zone without separate license

ArabLeague_OrangeCard.name: Arab League Orange Card
ArabLeague_OrangeCard.description: Cross-border motor insurance scheme for Arab League members in North Africa

majorPanAfricanProviders.name: BIMA
majorPanAfricanProviders.description: Digital microinsurance via mobile operators; present in Ghana, Tanzania, Senegal, and others
majorPanAfricanProviders.model: Freemium and paid via mobile money

majorPanAfricanProviders.name: MicroEnsure
majorPanAfricanProviders.description: Partners with telcos (MTN, Tigo, Airtel) to distribute microinsurance
majorPanAfricanProviders.model: Mobile-based premium collection via airtime/mobile money

majorPanAfricanProviders.name: Pula Advisors
majorPanAfricanProviders.description: Index-based agricultural insurance for smallholder farmers across Africa

majorPanAfricanProviders.name: Turaco
majorPanAfricanProviders.description: Embedded insurance for emerging markets, partners with digital platforms

distributionChannels: Mobile money (M-PESA, MTN Mobile Money, Orange Money, Airtel Money)
